patch.value: Reserved item-field name collisions are invisible to SDK lint/preflight/harness

An extension can register an item field named like a reserved front-matter key (e.g. 'severity'; FRONT_MATTER_KEY_ORDER) and pass assertExtensionPreflight + createExtensionTestHarness green, but pm create/update rejects the field at runtime — a green package can brick create. Fix: export RESERVED_ITEM_FIELD_NAMES from the SDK and validate registered item-field keys in lintExtensionBlueprint/preflightExtension/harness. Found in 2026-07-02 audit (Lane B, pm-6vy7).
SDK lint, preflight, and test harness each flag reserved item-field name collisions from the shared reserved-key list; a drift test keeps that list in sync with constants.

## Context
Found in the 2026-07-02 ecosystem audit (Lane B). An extension can register an item field whose name collides with a reserved front-matter key (e.g. 'severity', 'blocked_reason' — see FRONT_MATTER_KEY_ORDER) and still pass assertExtensionPreflight and createExtensionTestHarness green — but pm create/update rejects the field at runtime. A green package can brick create for its users.

## Approach
Export RESERVED_ITEM_FIELD_NAMES from the SDK (single source shared with the runtime validator) and validate registered item-field keys in lintExtensionBlueprint, preflightExtension, AND the test harness so the collision fails at authoring time with a rename hint. Known reserved names already bitten in the wild: severity (PR#150), blocked_reason (PR#367).

## Definition of done
A blueprint registering a reserved field name fails lint/preflight/harness with the same error code; SDK docs list the reserved names; runtime and SDK share one constant (no drift).
